posts.text: US district judge Paul Friedman sided with a New York Times in a lawsuit and struck down portions of a new pentagon policy that required journalists to sign an agreement that would restrict their ability to cover news independently. Most news outlets had refused to sign the policy and lost their credentials. This decision will allow them to submit for new credentials.  #pentagon #firstamendment #petehegseth
Tell me where you stand politically without actually telling me where you stand politically

Tulsi Gabbard, the Director of national intelligence, testified at a congressional about the intelligence community’s assessment of how imminent the danger from Iran was, to justify the attack by the United States.  The White House had announced that it had made the decision to strike Iran because of an “imminent threat”, but the intelligence community’s assessment seems to confirm the opposite.  ##iranwar##tulsigabbard

Is the save act disenfranchising women voters? A speech by Scott Yenor of the Heritage Foundation questions women’s rights, including their right to participate in their democratic process, and reveals the view that the organization has of women: a view that sees a woman’s role in society as secondary to men’s.  The SAVE Act makes it hard for millions of women to prove their right to vote.

Pete Hegseth doesn’t understand what patriotism is.  He suggested - through a video he shared - that people who complain about what’s wrong with America should just live.  No, Pete: that’s not what patriotism is. We complain because we love America. And we’ll stay and implement change because we love America.

Senator Mark Warner called the Iran a “war of choice”. His Senate Intelligence Committee has yet to receive any proof that there was imminent reason for the Pentagon to attack Iran.  And @teammarkwarner is right.  The decision created three set of problems: it replaced Iran’s leadership, it wasn’t authorized by Congress, and it’s costing millions of dollars an hour.

Donald Trump posted on social media a message to the Iranian soccer team, telling them that they should not come to the United States to play for the FIFA World Cup because their safety would be in danger.  After that message, the Iran team asked FIFA to move their game to Mexico, but FIFA rejected the request

After attacking Iran, Trump sets his sights on Cuba, telling the press that he can “do anything he wants” with Cuba. President Trump said that he can free Cuba or take it over. While “taking over” a sovereign nation is a violation of international law, this sentiment is the application of concepts that the president outlined in the “national security strategy“ document presented earlier this year to Congress.

The new talking point following the attacks by the US and Israel against Iran is that “Trump didn’t start that war. He ended a 47 year war.“ that is not the case for many reasons. Let’s fact check people like Vance Kay

Does Virginia House Bill 110 create a ban anywhere on Capitol Grounds but allows state lawmakers to avoid that ban? @The Older Millennial seems to think that, but that’s not at all what the bill says. The bill governs what needs to be locked in a car when someone parks on Capitol grounds. Let’s fact check him (again).
